titleEn: Day 29: A1 Full Review — All Skills
titleMn: Өдөр 29: A1 Бүрэн давталт — Бүх дадлага
objectiveEn: About one hour. Learn 20 review words, all A1 grammar review (am/is/are · can · Simple · Past · Going to), listening, speaking, homework.
objectiveMn: Ойролцоогоор 1 цаг. 20 шинэ үг, бүх A1 дүрмийн давталт, сонсох, ярих, гэрийн даалгавар.
contentEn: Review every grammar topic from the first 28 days while learning study and progress vocabulary.
contentMn: Эхний 28 хоногт сурсан бүх дүрмийг давтаж, суралцах ба ахицын тухай үгсийг сурна.

vocabulary.example: I improved a lot this month.
I want to be fluent in English.
I have a big vocabulary now.
I have four English skills now.
Correct your mistakes and move on.

quiz.question: I _____ English every day.
quiz.explanation: Present Simple for habits with 'I' uses base verb.

quiz.question: They _____ late — they were early!
quiz.explanation: Past tense negative for plural 'they' is 'weren't'.

quiz.question: He _____ come yesterday.
quiz.explanation: Past Simple negative uses 'didn't + base verb'.

quiz.question: How old is Bat?
quiz.explanation: Bat says 'I am 16 years old' in the listening.

quiz.question: Where is Bat from?
quiz.explanation: Bat says he is from Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia.

quiz.question: What happens tomorrow in the listening?
quiz.explanation: The teacher says 'See you tomorrow for Graduation Day!'

quiz.question: _____ you swim? Yes, I can!
quiz.explanation: Ability questions use 'Can you + base verb?'

page1.grammarTopic: All grammar review — am/is/are · can · Simple · Past · Going to
page1.grammarExplanation: Today we review ALL grammar from 28 days! A1 covers: To Be (am/is/are/was/were) · Present Simple (habits) · Present Continuous (now) · Can/Can't (ability) · There is/There are · Past Simple · Going to (future plans) · Possessive adjectives · Prepositions · Object pronouns. You have learned ALL of these — every single one! · Өнөөдөр 28 өдрийн БҮХ дүрмийг давтана! A1 түвшин: To Be · Present Simple · Present Continuous · Can/Can't · There is/are · Past Simple · Going to · бүх зүйлийг сурсан!

grammarTable.subject: To Be
grammarTable.positive: I am happy. She was tired.
grammarTable.question: Are you? Was she?

commonMistakes: I am go every day. → I GO every day. (habit = Simple)
Yesterday I go. → Yesterday I WENT. (past!)
She cans swim. → She CAN swim. (can never changes)
I am going to went. → I am going to GO. (base verb!)

page2.readingPassage: Today is Day 29 — the review day! Bat thinks about his English journey. One month ago, he couldn't say much in English. Now he can introduce himself, describe his family, talk about food, give directions, and discuss his plans. He studied every day and never gave up. Tomorrow is going to be the most special day — Graduation Day! Bat is so proud of himself!
speakingQuestions: 1. Tell me about yourself: name, age, family, job/school.
2. What do you do every day? (Present Simple)
3. What did you do yesterday? (Past Simple)
4. What are you going to do tomorrow? (Going to)
5. What can you do well in English now?
6. How much has your English improved in 29 days?

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: Let's review! What is your name and where are you from?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: My name is Bat. I am from Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: What do you do every day?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I study English every day and go to school.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: What did you do last weekend?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I went to the park and visited my grandparents.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: What can you do in English now and what are you going to do next year?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I can read, write, speak, and understand English! I am going to study at university and travel.

page2.speakingTip: Don't worry about mistakes — just keep talking! Every mistake helps you learn faster. Confidence is more important than perfection at this stage.

page3.listeningScript: Teacher: Today is our review day before graduation. Let's test everything! Bat, introduce yourself. Bat: My name is Bat. I am 16 years old. I am a student from Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia. Teacher: What did you do yesterday? Bat: I studied English for two hours and went for a walk in the park. Teacher: What are you going to do after graduation? Bat: I am going to start Book 2 and keep improving my English! Teacher: Can you speak English now? Bat: Yes I can! I can introduce myself, talk about my life, and have basic conversations! Teacher: I am so proud of you! See you tomorrow for Graduation Day!

listeningQuestions.question: How long did Bat study yesterday?
What else did Bat do yesterday?
What is Bat going to do after graduation?
What can Bat do in English?

homework: Writing: Write 8-10 sentences introducing yourself using ALL grammar — to be, present simple, past simple, going to, can.
Vocabulary: Review ALL 560 words from Days 1-28 — test yourself one last time!
Speaking: Practice saying: 'My name is... I am from... I study... Yesterday I... I can... I am going to...'.
Get ready: Tomorrow is Graduation Day! 🎓 You deserve to celebrate!
completionSummary: Completed FULL A1 review — ready for Day 30!
